description:
Fourth subticket of fz-ul4.10. Depends on .10.3 (expansion pass).

Without hygiene, a macro that introduces a local `x` will shadow any `x` in the caller's scope. Elixir's solution is gensym + a context tag.

Approach for v1: when a macro returns AST containing a Var node `{name, meta, ctx}`, rename name to a unique gensym (e.g. `name__macro_<n>`) UNLESS the var came from an unquoted caller-side expression (which carries the caller's ctx).

Defer until .10.3 lands and we can see real failure modes.
title: Macro hygiene (gensym)
